_purpose: Pool of strategies, starters, assessments and Ghanaian contexts that the AI draws from randomly to ensure every generated lesson is different from the last.

starter_types.question_and_answer: Ask learners probing questions to activate prior knowledge about [topic]
starter_types.song_or_rhyme: Teach or sing a song related to [topic] with actions
starter_types.video_or_picture: Show a short video clip or pictures related to [topic] and invite comments
starter_types.game: Play a quick classroom game such as [game_name] to introduce [topic]
starter_types.real_object: Display a real object related to [topic] and ask learners what they notice
starter_types.story: Tell a short Ghanaian story or folktale that introduces the concept of [topic]
starter_types.brain_teaser: Pose a riddle or brain teaser connected to [topic]
starter_types.recall: Ask learners to recall and share what they know from the previous lesson
starter_types.demonstration: Demonstrate a simple activity related to [topic] and ask learners what they observe

assessment_types.oral_questioning: Ask individual learners to answer questions orally to check understanding
assessment_types.written_exercise: Give learners [3-5] written questions to complete individually
assessment_types.group_task: Assign a collaborative task where groups present their answers to the class
assessment_types.practical_demonstration: Ask learners to demonstrate the skill or concept learnt
assessment_types.peer_assessment: Learners exchange work and assess each other using a given checklist
assessment_types.exit_ticket: Learners write one thing they learned and one question they still have
assessment_types.class_game: Play a competitive game such as 'Back to the Board' or 'Quiz Quiz Trade'
assessment_types.portfolio: Learners add their completed work to their portfolio for teacher review

foods: fried yam and garden egg sauce

phase1_openers: Play the video on [topic] for learners to watch.
Show pictures related to [topic] and ask learners to describe what they see.
Ask learners to name as many [topic-related items] as possible in 60 seconds.
Sing a familiar song related to [topic] with learners.
Tell a short story about [Ghanaian name] who experienced [topic] in real life.
Play a quick brain-warming game: [describe game].
Show a real object and ask: 'What do you notice? What questions do you have?'
Through questions and answers, revise the previous lesson.
Engage learners in a think-pair-share: 'What do you already know about [topic]?'
Display a chart/diagram and ask learners to observe and comment.
phase3_closers: What have we learnt today? Ask learners to summarize the main points.
Have learners to summarize the important points of the lesson.
Use series of questions and answers to review learners' understanding.
Call learners in turns to summarize the lesson.
Give learners task to complete while you go round the class to support those who need extra help.
Engage pupils in a think-pair-share activity to recap key learning.
Ask learners: 'What did you find difficult? What will you remember?'
Ask learners to talk about how today's lesson will help them in daily life.
Take feedback from learners and summarize key points on the board.
Call on individual learners to explain the lesson in their own words.
